body.title: CLOSED: Dedicated slow_queries.log file in db_proxy
body.result: lazy RotatingFileHandler writes slow_queries.log on first slow query; path respects IGOR_LOG_DIR; 1 test added; 4492 TheIgors tests green
body.description:
Slow queries currently emit WARNING to the general log stream (logger=lab.utility_closet.db_proxy). They should also write to a dedicated slow_queries.log so performance regressions are easy to spot without grepping through the main log. Each entry: timestamp, elapsed_ms, sql (truncated), caller module if inferrable.

**Affected files:** lab/utility_closet/db_proxy.py (slow query detection lives in _db_log or _on_sql), ~/.TheIgors/logs/ (output location)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; log goes through RotatingFileHandler pattern already used by logging_setup.py
**Scope boundary:** add dedicated file handler for slow-query lines only; do NOT change the existing WARNING log path (keep both). No schema changes. No new tool/habit needed — file is readable directly or via get_slow_query_report.
**Test plan:** add test asserting slow query writes to the dedicated file path; existing slow-query tests must still pass
